# v1.6.2

## Features

- None.

## Bug Fixes

- Fixed installed MDE builds so Editor Agent Chat no longer disappears just because a GUI-launched app inherits a sparse `PATH`.
- Added a desktop main-process Agent Chat runner that resolves the user's login-shell `PATH` for Codex capability probes and sustained `codex app-server --listen stdio://` sessions.
- Preserved structured Agent Chat diagnostics for missing Codex, authentication-required states, unsupported app-server protocol states, and sanitized IPC availability responses.
- Bounded lightweight Codex capability probes so installed-app detection cannot hang indefinitely on `codex --version`, login-status, or app-server help checks.

## Breaking Changes

- None.

## Maintenance

- Bumped workspace release metadata to `1.6.2` for the root package, desktop app, and changed Agent Chat package.
- Added unit, integration, and packaged-path E2E coverage for sparse GUI `PATH`, login-shell Codex discovery, sustained app-server spawning, engine-missing diagnostics, protocol-unsupported diagnostics, and authentication-required availability.

## Documentation/User Manual

- Updated the zh-CN AI manual to clarify installed-app Agent Chat visibility and login-shell Codex discovery.
- Updated the zh-CN troubleshooting manual with hidden Agent Chat entry causes and diagnostic guidance.

## Verification

- `pnpm run lint`
- `pnpm run typecheck`
- `pnpm run build`
- `pnpm run test:unit`
- `pnpm run test:integration`
- `pnpm run test:e2e`
- `pnpm run docs:build`
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm --filter @mde/agent-chat test`
- `pnpm --filter @mde/agent-chat typecheck`
- `pnpm --filter @mde/agent-chat lint`
- `pnpm --filter @mde/agent-chat build`
- Manual divergent testing covered release-like packaged sparse `PATH`, real logged-in Codex Agent Chat turns in the built app, unsupported/auth-required states, response latency, visual behavior, and adjacent AI action behavior.

## Artifacts

- GitHub Release workflow should publish macOS DMG/ZIP artifacts and Windows NSIS updater artifacts for `v1.6.2`.
- Optional macOS signing and notarization secrets are not required for the no-paid-account release path; macOS artifacts may be unsigned if signing secrets are absent.
- Final release validation must smoke-test the actual GitHub-published macOS artifact before the bug task is moved to done.
